message:
feat(logging): add visual indicators to log output functions

Add Unicode visual indicators to logging functions for improved visual
clarity and consistency across installer output:

- log_success: Adds green checkmark (✓) prefix for success messages
- log_warn: Adds yellow warning symbol (⚠) prefix for warnings
- log_error: Adds red cross (✖) prefix for error messages

These visual indicators make it easier to scan installer output and
quickly identify the status of each logged message. The existing color
coding is preserved; this change adds semantic icons that work well
even when colors are stripped (e.g., in log files).

This aligns with the console output patterns documented in AGENTS.md
where progress/status messages use distinct visual markers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -64,23 +64,23 @@ fi
 # Usage: log_success "Installation complete"
 if ! declare -f log_success >/dev/null; then
     log_success() {
-        printf "${ACFS_GREEN}%s${ACFS_NC}\n" "$1" >&2
+        printf "${ACFS_GREEN}✓ %s${ACFS_NC}\n" "$1" >&2
     }
 fi
 
 # Log warning message (yellow with warning symbol)
 # Usage: log_warn "This may take a while"
 if ! declare -f log_warn >/dev/null; then
     log_warn() {
-        printf "${ACFS_YELLOW}%s${ACFS_NC}\n" "$1" >&2
+        printf "${ACFS_YELLOW}⚠ %s${ACFS_NC}\n" "$1" >&2
     }
 fi
 
 # Log error message (red with X)
 # Usage: log_error "Failed to install package"
 if ! declare -f log_error >/dev/null; then
     log_error() {
-        printf "${ACFS_RED}%s${ACFS_NC}\n" "$1" >&2
+        printf "${ACFS_RED}✖ %s${ACFS_NC}\n" "$1" >&2
     }
 fi
